nameMeanings.name: Cochran
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and Renfrewshire): habitational name from the lands of Cochrane in the parish of Paisley, near Glasgow. The placename is of uncertain derivation, perhaps from Welsh coch ‘red’, although this etymology is not supported by the early spelling Coueran.

variants.name: Cothran
meanings: Perhaps also English: variant of   Cawthorne  .

variants.name: Coughran
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cochran  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cothren
meanings: Perhpas alsoa variant of English   Cawthorne  .

variants.name: Cochren
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cochran  . This name is extinct in Britain.

variants.name: Cochrum
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Cochran   or English   Cockerham  .

variants.name: Caughron
meanings: Shortened and altered form of Irish   McCaghren  .

variants.name: Cothron
meanings: Perhaps also English: variant of   Cawthorne  .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Alan John Cochran was born on 25 September 1432, in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, William Cochrane, was 67 and his mother, Mary Lindsay, was 67. He had at least 1 son with Margaret Salisbury. He died in 1484, in his hometown, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alan John Cochran est né le 25 septembre 1432, à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Cochrane, avait 67 ans et sa mère, Mary Lindsay, avait 67 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaret Salisbury. Il est décédé en 1484, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alan John Cochran am 25. September 1432 in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Cochrane 67 und seine Mutter Mary Lindsay 67. Er hatte mit Margaret Salisbury mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1484 im Alter von 52 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alan John Cochran è nato il il 25 settembre 1432 nel Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Cochrane, aveva 67 anni e il suo madre, Mary Lindsay, aveva 67 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Margaret Salisbury. Morì nel 1484, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 52 anni.
